functor_compose_axiom[O1, M1, O2, M2](f.src_cat, f.dst_cat, f.obj_map, f.mor_map) = forall(x0: M1, x1: M1) { f.src_cat.src(x0) = f.src_cat.dst(x1) implies f.mor_map(f.src_cat.compose(x0, x1)) = f.dst_cat.compose(f.mor_map(x0), f.mor_map(x1)) }

goal: functor_compose_axiom[O, M, O, M](c, c, identity_fn[O], identity_fn[M])
proof: function[T0, T1, T3, T5](x0: Category[T1, T0], x1: T0 -> T3, x2: Category[T5, T3], x3: T1 -> T5) { forall(x4: T0, x5: T0) { x0.src(x4) != x0.dst(x5) or x1(x0.compose(x4, x5)) = x2.compose(x1(x4), x1(x5)) } = functor_compose_axiom[T1, T0, T5, T3](x0, x2, x3, x1) }[M, O, M, O](c, identity_fn[M], c, identity_fn[O])

let w1: M satisfy { c.src(w0) = c.dst(w1) and identity_fn(c.compose(w0, w1)) != c.compose(identity_fn(w0), identity_fn(w1)) }
let w2: M satisfy { c.src(w0) = c.dst(w2) and identity_fn(c.compose(w0, w2)) != c.compose(identity_fn(w0), identity_fn(w2)) }
function(x0: M, x1: M) { c.dst(x0) != c.src(x1) or c.compose(identity_fn(x1), identity_fn(x0)) = identity_fn(c.compose(x1, x0)) }(w2, w0)

proof: function[T0, T1, T2, T5](x0: T2 -> T1, x1: Category[T2, T0], x2: Category[T1, T5], x3: T0 -> T5) { forall(x4: T0) { x0(x1.src(x4)) = x2.src(x3(x4)) } = functor_src_axiom[T2, T0, T1, T5](x1, x2, x0, x3) }[M1, O3, O1, M3](compose[O1, O2, O3](f.obj_map, g.obj_map), g.src_cat, f.dst_cat, compose[M1, M2, M3](f.mor_map, g.mor_map))

proof: function[T0, T1, T3, T5](x0: Category[T1, T0], x1: T0 -> T3, x2: Category[T5, T3], x3: T1 -> T5) { forall(x4: T0, x5: T0) { x0.src(x4) != x0.dst(x5) or x1(x0.compose(x4, x5)) = x2.compose(x1(x4), x1(x5)) } = functor_compose_axiom[T1, T0, T5, T3](x0, x2, x3, x1) }[M1, O1, M3, O3](g.src_cat, compose[M1, M2, M3](f.mor_map, g.mor_map), f.dst_cat, compose[O1, O2, O3](f.obj_map, g.obj_map))
